question: Will the clinic's monthly referrals recover to their December level by mid-October 2025?

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

clinic_decision_authority.evidence: Sam Whitfield holds sole authority over fees and programs; Kim proposes only.

fee_reversal_implementation_time.rule: Fee reversal takes effect at start of next quarter after decision

partner_program_setup_time.rule: Partner program requires 2-4 weeks to stand up after decision

referral_source_stats_availability.rule: Kim can pull referral-source stats within one week of request

quarterly_budget.constraints: Fee reversal costs 8k/quarter; partner program costs 5k setup

referral_source_visibility.rule: Practice manager can see monthly referral counts per source via practice management system

gp_reason_unknown.rule: GP Alvarez's reason for stopping referrals is unknown until explicitly asked

sam_whitfield.why_consequential: Holds sole authority over fees and programs; decides whether to reverse fee or approve partner program
affordances: ask GP Alvarez for reason for stopping referrals

referring_gp_alvarez.why_consequential: Was top referrer (25/month); stopped in February; resumption directly impacts recovery to December baseline
affordances: provide reason for stopping when asked

practice_manager_kim.why_consequential: Can pull referral stats and propose interventions; cannot decide

unresolved_mechanisms: GP Alvarez's reason for stopping referrals (unknown until asked)
Partner program setup duration (2-4 weeks uncertain)
Whether fee reversal would actually cause GP Alvarez to resume referring
Whether any other GPs would increase referrals to compensate
assumptions: December baseline of 60/month is the target for recovery
No other GPs started or stopped referring between February and prediction time
Fee reversal and partner program are the only interventions available
Prediction horizon 2025-09-16 means we assess whether recovery is on track by that date

auto_repairs: institution 'clinic_decision_authority' aggregation defaulted to single_authority (none declared; labeled assumed)

missing_decisive_elements: No entity for the clinic's actual December 2024 referral count; the target is 60 but the question asks about recovery to 'December level', which may differ from the target.
No entity or fact for the clinic's current monthly referral count trajectory (e.g., counts for March–September 2025) to assess whether recovery is plausible by mid-October.
No entity for the specific GP Alvarez's referral history (e.g., was he the only top referrer? Did others also stop?).
No entity for the clinic's capacity to handle increased referrals (e.g., staffing, appointment slots) which could constrain recovery even if GPs resume.
No entity for external factors (e.g., competing clinics, policy changes, seasonal effects) that could affect referral volumes.
relabeled_progress_bars: The process 'referral_recovery_assessment' uses a binary threshold of 60 referrals, but the question asks about recovery to 'December level' — if December was higher than 60, the bar is mislabeled.

The transition from 'decision_made' to 'recovery_achieved' requires both gp_resumes_referring AND monthly_referral_count >= 60, but the model does not track which GPs resume or how many referrals they generate individually.
hidden_answer_risks: The model assumes that only GP Alvarez matters for recovery, but other GPs may have stopped or started referring, and their aggregate effect is not modeled.
The model assumes that a director decision (approve fee reversal or partner program) will lead to GP resumption, but there is no evidence that Alvarez will resume even if the director acts.
The model does not account for the time lag between a decision and actual referral resumption — mid-October may be too soon even if a decision is made in September.
The model does not include any mechanism for the clinic to actively recruit new referring GPs to compensate for lost referrals.
direct_human_reaction_encodings: Sam Whitfield's affordance 'ask GP Alvarez for reason for stopping referrals' is listed but no corresponding event or process transition uses that affordance — it is a dead end.
Practice manager Kim's affordances are limited to proposing and pulling stats, but there is no encoding of Kim's ability to negotiate or persuade the director or GPs.
GP Alvarez's affordance 'provide reason for stopping when asked' is not linked to any decision or recovery outcome — the reason is never used in the model.
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: The model treats 'monthly_referral_count' as an objective fact, but the clinic might publicly report a different number (e.g., to stakeholders) than the internal count.
The model assumes that a 'decision_record' reflects the true decision, but the director might privately decide differently than what is recorded (e.g., approve but delay implementation).
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: No mechanism for automated referral tracking systems (e.g., EHR software) that might generate referral counts with delays or errors.
No mechanism for insurance or billing changes that could affect referral patterns independently of GP behavior.
No mechanism for seasonal or calendar effects (e.g., holidays, flu season) that influence referral volumes in October versus December.
